name: CiteMed Docs Portal
description: Confluence-synced documentation portal with magic-link auth and email contact form.

SECRET_KEY.description: Django SECRET_KEY. Generate with: python -c 'import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(64))'

ALLOWED_HOSTS.description: Comma-separated hostnames this app responds to (e.g. docs.example.com).

ADMIN_PATH.description: URL path for Django admin (no slashes). Defaults to 'admin'. Set to something unguessable in production to keep the admin out of bot scans.

CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS.description: Comma-separated origins trusted for CSRF (e.g. https://docs.example.com).

FRONTEND_URL.description: Public URL — used to build magic-link URLs in emails (e.g. https://docs.example.com).

CONFLUENCE_DOMAIN.description: Atlassian instance host, no protocol (e.g. citemed.atlassian.net).

CONFLUENCE_EMAIL.description: Email of the Atlassian user whose API token is used for sync.

ATLASSIAN_CLOUD_ID.description: Cloud ID for the Atlassian site (required by Trinity client).

MAILGUN_ACCESS_KEY.description: Mailgun API key. When set, EMAIL_BACKEND is switched to django_mailgun.MailgunBackend.

SUPPORT_EMAIL.description: Destination for the contact form (e.g. support@citemed.com).

cron.comment: Backstop for the Mailgun delivery webhook — reconciles any events it missed (ECD-2248).
Option A: create + link a Jira issue for open unlinked tickets via the API (ECD-2246). No-op unless JIRA_AUTO_CREATE is set.

Pull public service-desk Jira comments into linked portal tickets so replies typed in Jira reach the customer (S1, ECD-2246). Every minute; for ~30s near-real-time run 'sync_jira_comments --loop --interval 30' as a worker instead.
